title: Profile update does not invalidate Redis cache; reads serve stale data for up to 5 minutes
preconditions: User U's profile is cached under redis key profile:U with TTL 300 s.
Less than 300 s have elapsed since the cache was populated.
steps: (api-caller) PUT /v1/profile with display_name='Alice (new)' as user U
(api-caller) GET /v1/profile/U immediately afterwards (within the TTL window)
(observer) Inspect the response body display_name and compare against the database row
expected: GET /v1/profile/U returns display_name='Alice (new)' (the value just written).
actual: GET /v1/profile/U returns the previously-cached display_name for up to 300 s, until the TTL expires or the key is evicted.
suggested_fix_direction: On PUT /v1/profile, after the DB update succeeds, call redis.delete(f'profile:{user_id}') (write-invalidate cache-aside). Alternatively write-through: update redis with the new value in the same transaction.
